subject: These shouldn’t be essential school supplies:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MCGUIGAN FOR PA
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28854377/284542908/-1233952322?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MjcwNGNhMi0wNmIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fxc5YR76mFoBtkKIdvhMhHR-EXJ49F8LPMcw9tQOhRs=&emci=319f3d4f-89af-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&emdi=52704ca2-06b0-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&ceid=18185356)
---

| | Peter – **Books.** **Notebooks.** **And ...a bulletproof backpack??** I am a father of three, two of whom attend public high school. No parent should worry about their child being killed at school; unfortunately, as we return to in-school classes, school shootings have also returned. On average, over the last decade, 12 kids a year go to school and never return home, 24 are wounded, and hundreds more are traumatized. This is another problem politicians would rather tweet about than solve. Our gun violence epidemic is so out of hand that some parents _outfit their kids with bulletproof backpacks_. **How do I know this? Because I am one of them.** **[Chip in $16 right now and continue building this grassroots U.S. Senate campaign. Together, let's do something about our country's gun violence problem.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28854380/284542911/-34735242?refcode=2021-05-08-backpacks&recurring=0&amount=16&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=52704ca2-06b0-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MjcwNGNhMi0wNmIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fxc5YR76mFoBtkKIdvhMhHR-EXJ49F8LPMcw9tQOhRs=)** This isn't about freedom of anything except the freedom of our kids and teachers to be safe, and of parents to not fear that goodbyes before school might be their last ones. **Yes, I support the constitutional right to bear arms, but that doesn't mean I think that the assault rifles, actual weapons of war, should be easier to buy than _lawn darts._** The NRA would have you believe that guns have rights. That's ridiculous. My child's right to live – or your child's right – is more important to me than gun rights.**** **Yes, I said that.** Politicians like Pat Toomey, funded by the NRA and afraid of their influence, have failed time and again to address this public health issue which has spiraled out of control. Let's build a different kind of campaign, a movement to have real conversations about this issue in Washington D.C. – to protect our children, their teachers, and our families, and build a brighter, safer future. ... and then make _real_ , long-overdue changes. **[Chip in $16 today and let's get it done together.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28854381/284542913/-34735242?refcode=2021-05-08-backpacks&recurring=0&amount=16&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=52704ca2-06b0-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI1MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MjcwNGNhMi0wNmIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fxc5YR76mFoBtkKIdvhMhHR-EXJ49F8LPMcw9tQOhRs=)** name: James Carville
subject: We might lose in Virginia, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Terry for Virginia
body:
They’re outraising us
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28855624/284547321/-2023814932?refcode=20210508_JC_5JCO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGE3MmQ2Zi0wOWIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_n_LB3EWffDaLx_w7lq2024ojPfCzDF0nAZkADRNYnA=)

| | Derek,

I’ve sounded the alarm before about self-proclaimed “Trump in Heels” Amanda Chase. But here’s an important update:

**Amanda Chase is still leading in the polls, Republicans are outraising Terry, and I’m scared to death that we might LOSE.** We’ve got to get behind Terry right now.

**[Will you donate right now to help my friend Terry McAuliffe stop Amanda Chase?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28855625/284547322/-2023814932?refcode=20210508_JC_5JCO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGE3MmQ2Zi0wOWIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_n_LB3EWffDaLx_w7lq2024ojPfCzDF0nAZkADRNYnA=)**

She says it herself, she’s “Trump in heels.” Doesn’t believe in science, spews racism and hate, and spreads conspiracy theories that undermine our democracy.

We’ve got to rally behind Terry to stop her, Derek.

I’ve worked side-by-side with Terry as he’s beaten extremist Republicans, and he’ll do it again. name: Derek Kilmer
subject: Can you do this for me, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
Peter,

I'm certain that America's best days are ahead of us, but the only way we strengthen our country is by moving forward **together.**

We have to heal the division that has sadly become a hallmark of American politics. We have to aid communities, small businesses, and working families in the slow march to COVID-19 recovery. And we have to help President Joe Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris build our nation back better, to make sure the issues of working people are a priority.

That's why I'm reaching out. One of my favorite parts about my job is hearing directly from the folks I serve. I want to hear from you, Peter, so I can learn what's at the top of your list as we embark on this next chapter.

[**It 's a privilege to serve you and the home that we love, which is why it's so important I know what's on your mind. Please take a moment to let me know how I can best serve you and your family in Congress. Click here to take our quick, one-minute survey.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/9V2xljVCCLyvX-hnN9NfGSKK2IS18Ug56QlNZKQjunM=/adROeU5HKH0Y5-_BCd-Ec-NJxPi-Lq6AOMbWW59CaHg=?gpsid=39_115_16543&ms=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&refcode=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&utm_campaign=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dkil)

Despite winning the presidency, the hard work has only just started. The past administration did a lot of damage, and our country has some healing to do. Even now, reading the news can be overwhelming. **That 's why we need to hear from each other.**

The key to any healthy relationship is communication. Knowing what you care about and your views on the most important issues is vital to our efforts to conquer the biggest challenges together in the coming months.

[**So please, if you have a free moment, I would really appreciate you taking our survey and sharing your thoughts.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/9V2xljVCCLyvX-hnN9NfGSKK2IS18Ug56QlNZKQjunM=/adROeU5HKH0Y5-_BCd-Ec-NJxPi-Lq6AOMbWW59CaHg=?gpsid=39_115_16543&ms=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&refcode=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&utm_campaign=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dkil)

It's an honor to serve you,

Derek

[DONATE](https://act.myngp.com/el/9V2xljVCCLyvX-hnN9NfGSKK2IS18Ug56QlNZKQjunM=/X02YSoc9P31fFKKxpxhVUXaD1P6fSmk5bAvkCSPQ_C0=?gpsid=52_115_16359&refcode=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&utm_campaign=em20_210312_onbrd_engg_gq_cust_ws2_DK_ettx&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dkil)

| Dear Patriot, I have a shocking update. **Last week I told you about Joe Biden's attempts to circumvent the Constitution and spy on everything you do.** But now the details are coming out, and it's even worse than I imagined. **As it turns out, conservatives really are the top target. And even the private messages that you send on social media aren't safe.** According to new reports, Joe Biden's spy agencies are partnering up with Big Tech companies to collect information on what conservatives -- or as they like to call us, "domestic terrorists" -- are saying to each other on social media. I wish I didn’t have such dire news to share with you, but your Constitutional rights are in more danger today than they have been in a very long time. After all, we know exactly who Biden's spy state is going to use its enormous power to hunt down -- conservatives. These agencies are too powerful to begin with, and now that power is aimed directly at conservative patriots like you and me. [Please Patriot, if you want to help me stop the Left from targeting innocent conservative patriots like you, please contribute $100, $50, $25, or $10 immediately. It's extremely important.](https://trk.klclick1.com/ls/click?upn=02bixpj5NQJZaKcsmXY64eerP7KIEnfA2Pj-2F3zl5lv-2BlR2U7omAhGtUUZGUazUSv34vrsZhsMFPmr4rYFmgJaABun4Nz8z198ra83JqyrUpCm4FwPpYQ3DWLIWLk-2Fxv73ngcNUzY4zJCcRCAcL7eOrHXKoyM00SdCdqSD79hWixApROneEzOF3NXldQMp-2Byk2t5b_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymjdyn9Meo-2BU4memdpyMXxi7FBJlx8D43HWh-2FwvuMbgX0nDiR6eWZVC-2B6QPZxGk3TzFYZ7UmsFD-2BT6eKi6G-2B0NJQCPHsoDCDVV6ulJvP1J6917dAZxo-2FrGz3OYdtsz4ooNodeAbhLbsNa4SbPdbc4phY96zCLHCiMvYy7xdG1dDjxXhqG0IwRUI2MMlADuhxYtjoPs1aSaKA2Gvb7s3ulYE2R7L4C68L-2BzDgb-2FGZqR9djoAR-2FlMK3R9Oszim5ZvTzGHcBX7wcJM0RNIqR5eRAASh1Wq-2F-2BZWNCq3a5yJGfg-2FEgFXQO6-2BR6LvT188u4CftdP2I5HTo2bvjvqrfz1lx27dbsne0r7u-2Bz4CySbkXjUHEhg-3D-3D) This is still America. You have freedom of speech. You can think, believe, and say whatever you want, and the government has no right to target or harass you for it. Those are the principles America was founded on, and those are the principles I was elected to the U.S. Senate to uphold. But now that Biden is using the most secretive intelligence agencies to target people for their views online, those values are all in jeopardy. **The Constitution truly is hanging in the balance, and I do not say that lightly.** If America becomes a nation where every word that you say online -- even in private -- is monitored by the Feds, and those who speak out of line are labeled as "terrorists" and targeted, then we are in an extremely dangerous place. I have introduced legislation along with 19 other Senators to stop this awful power grab by Joe Biden, but with so much pressure to scrap the Fourth Amendment from members of BOTH parties, building support for it is going to be a challenge. That's why I need your help more than ever before. I can't let Joe Biden seize any more power than he already has. I have to stop him. clean_body: | | | | | | | Dear Patriot, I have a shocking update. Last week I told you about Joe Biden's attempts to circumvent the Constitution and spy on everything you do. But now the details are coming out, and it's even worse than I imagined. As it turns out, conservatives really are the top target. And even the private messages that you send on social media aren't safe. According to new reports, Joe Biden's spy agencies are partnering up with Big Tech companies to collect information on what conservatives -- or as they like to call us, "domestic terrorists" -- are saying to each other on social media. I wish I didn’t have such dire news to share with you, but your Constitutional rights are in more danger today than they have been in a very long time. After all, we know exactly who Biden's spy state is going to use its enormous power to hunt down -- conservatives. These agencies are too powerful to begin with, and now that power is aimed directly at conservative patriots like you and me. This is still America. You have freedom of speech. You can think, believe, and say whatever you want, and the government has no right to target or harass you for it. Those are the principles America was founded on, and those are the principles I was elected to the U.S. Senate to uphold. But now that Biden is using the most secretive intelligence agencies to target people for their views online, those values are all in jeopardy. The Constitution truly is hanging in the balance, and I do not say that lightly. If America becomes a nation where every word that you say online -- even in private -- is monitored by the Feds, and those who speak out of line are labeled as "terrorists" and targeted, then we are in an extremely dangerous place. I have introduced legislation along with 19 other Senators to stop this awful power grab by Joe Biden, but with so much pressure to scrap the Fourth Amendment from members of BOTH parties, building support for it is going to be a challenge. That's why I need your help more than ever before. I can't let Joe Biden seize any more power than he already has. I have to stop him. America must not allow a lawless regime to destroy our way of life. In Liberty, Rand Paul | | ****** Senator Rand Paul (R-KY) | | **** P.S. Joe Biden's lawless regime is labeling you a "domestic terrorist," and they're teaming up with Big Tech to monitor what you say on social media and target you if you step out of line. I will do anything I can to protect you -- and other like-minded conservative patriots -- from this un-American violation of our Constitutional rights. But in order to stop them, I need your help. You and I can't let Joe Biden seize any more power than he already has. ** | | | | | | Paid for by Rand Paul for US Senate | No longer want to receive these emails? . RANDPAC PO Box 72598 Newport, Kentucky 41072

name: Blaine, GOP News, Me (3)
subject: Do you agree with the Big Government spending spree?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blaine For Congress
body:
Congressman Luetkemeyer wanted us to reach out and see if you stand with Conservatives or the corporate elite who continue to bend the knee to anything the Democrats whine about.

It's Congressman Luetkemeyer. I know you are busy but I need to ask you a very important question.

As Joe Biden moves past 100 days in office as President, Democrats are still operating on full power to push ahead with forcing their socialist agenda onto America.

Yes, the American Taxpayer paid the bills for the emergency spending programs that helped our country coordinate its national response to get us where we are today.

**But in almost every case,** the personal restrictions put on Americans by the government went too far and allowed the idea of higher taxes and more government control over Americans' everyday lives to grow popular.

It's Congressman Luetkemeyer. I know you are busy but I need to ask you a very important question. Yes, the American Taxpayer paid the bills for the emergency spending programs that helped our country coordinate its national response to get us where we are today. But in almost every case, the personal restrictions put on Americans by the government went too far and allowed the idea of higher taxes and more government control over Americans' everyday lives to grow popular. clean_body: Joe Biden is completely beholden to the radical environmentalists in Congress. Fellow Conservative, Joe Biden in his address to Congress last week claimed that his radical environmental agenda was all about jobs. Well, fellow conservative, this is just a bunch of lies. In reality, one of his first acts as president was to cancel construction on the Keystone XL Pipeline and destroy good-paying oil and gas jobs. These decisions will hurt real Americans who are just trying to support their families. == Fight for American Jobs: Chip in Any Amount NOW! ** == == == == == Joe Biden is completely beholden to the radical environmentalists in Congress, and it is putting people's livelihoods on the line. And make no mistake, if we let the radical socialists in Congress ram through the Green New Deal, there will be millions of more jobs lost. We need patriots like you to step up tonight to put an end to these job crushing policies. The Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC) isn't just another alphabet soup political committee. We're the largest caucus of Republican state leaders in the nation with one unified goal: winning. subject: Big Tech has gone too far.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
They want to force the Silent Majority to remain silent forever. We can't let them win.

************
[](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001c4NAsC6xgpPpb2L5R9kPFcpT1LGQIRn66qXTpjSv7K-zzfOeiPG6Kl1kUQZiZzjL7uOdaASFejHI41oQpka329mEbslJjZoYccjMlGo89yb6Lo-Uri0UOlwhjfOC0Oen5paXVazYHb4Ut7Hg8EbcmZJJ9dZ8retUEWPSEOg1zI9nxMeoy8oh5Tj0tw4xl6e0LLcEumRgJ_hluXtXLdYS7Sl_YW7GwdKGus6H2x4ntKeG9JRfubFfTmTinNBBrOJwNAAv8rvRzDeZU1Mq_uTSIEqR9aOKDZsmvTW727_Fhn2M5kDz5Fx1_bRW6YHPLraAVOXH3Up6pUdTmELyaQbZ5KW6jaQXQ4PpWX1ZGbEJBTkx959DshYKy9TFXMM63vWt3IYUnAPQOZMWKwQTEZB-lN4FyGl0euXezvqTTSzsEZ8mxRu_DOja0qtuNQaKIXF3&c=9VIoq7j6WZ2NNrA6GIubNIs78zXRA7EvV2ohV1HUvO-CjayQgGZNCg==&ch=UICppeFtBv1mWPIBhVQcNDZBKTUrTd8ZwN0tzpXRE_MT0y5wC9Q0SA==)

[**STAND WITH TRUMP**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001c4NAsC6xgpPpb2L5R9kPFcpT1LGQIRn66qXTpjSv7K-zzfOeiPG6Kl1kUQZiZzjL7uOdaASFejHI41oQpka329mEbslJjZoYccjMlGo89yb6Lo-Uri0UOlwhjfOC0Oen5paXVazYHb4Ut7Hg8EbcmZJJ9dZ8retUEWPSEOg1zI9nxMeoy8oh5Tj0tw4xl6e0LLcEumRgJ_hluXtXLdYS7Sl_YW7GwdKGus6H2x4ntKeG9JRfubFfTmTinNBBrOJwNAAv8rvRzDeZU1Mq_uTSIEqR9aOKDZsmvTW727_Fhn2M5kDz5Fx1_bRW6YHPLraAVOXH3Up6pUdTmELyaQbZ5KW6jaQXQ4PpWX1ZGbEJBTkx959DshYKy9TFXMM63vWt3IYUnAPQOZMWKwQTEZB-lN4FyGl0euXezvqTTSzsEZ8mxRu_DOja0qtuNQaKIXF3&c=9VIoq7j6WZ2NNrA6GIubNIs78zXRA7EvV2ohV1HUvO-CjayQgGZNCg==&ch=UICppeFtBv1mWPIBhVQcNDZBKTUrTd8ZwN0tzpXRE_MT0y5wC9Q0SA==)

Fellow Conservative, it's Newt Gingrich here.

With the news of Facebook upholding their ban on President Trump, it's time we step up to Big Tech _**together**_.

Liberal Tech Billionaires in Silicon Valley have tried to silence conservative voices for YEARS. They HATE President Trump and his America First supporters. They'll do anything and everything they can to SILENCE us and DESTROY President Trump's legacy -- even if that means violating our right to free speech.

Marijuana legalization is a matter of racial justice and human rights. Peter: Legalizing cannabis is a matter of criminal justice reform and racial justice; we can no longer tolerate hundreds of thousands of men and women being torn from their families for nonviolent marijuana offences. As a State Senator, marijuana legalization has been one of Sen. Street’s top priorities, and he’s worked hard to reform our criminal justice system that too often perpetuates systems of violence. subject: President Trump’s new platform

body:
Planned Parenthood illegally stole $80 million of taxpayer dollars to use for their abortion services
|
---
| | |
Planned Parenthood illegally stole $80 million of taxpayer dollars to use for their abortion services, Friend.

**This is appalling.** The Paycheck Protection Program was not meant to fund abortions. I cannot believe that it was abused in this manner. I am shocked and disappointed.

That is why Planned Parenthood must return the $80 million dollars they **STOLE** from taxpayers. We have opened up our petition, DEMANDING they return the money. _**[Will you take 2 seconds to sign it -- it'd mean a ton if we could reach 10,000 signatures!](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001GigaGr4A92hqsw8tkEY7UJWCziipjPer1beXVN6W1rlz_12Z_FrmRq5ZhFz6vr3mG7WZTaZgY733X2hcwWM_ldm6bGAqXOzEGf9HfOfnwySeJgCxYZtfgvxOICEU-wiIYA1WBalc4wiT9cdN9Xv97xN-s3_6ld5tKpEKkTeIS0FCSGiv9K7rQRlSbtKlIqOJYjUuVUifBOOh_fZpvtf-6czo3sswgpAfcXxa2Skvw3bi-e_JF17unFWXcRsY6KDuqSjvihXkH5-LgVnzOhiyyDa1pA0ZMlEIxCeFvkLnrsM_62kPlPKO9KJDvsNCvLzzrfZ7r-56BMXdj6ZszYbwFoV_6No6JFfrkLuJT1zHGnCEKi-kESWsmTSwYpvUre-kPUyNrwbKo9t6b_RTx4dLnw==&c=Xv_b0l62GfzO5y2A1T_H3Z0NyqrmBgUILvXO050f0QaX0Shi8cxZpw==&ch=kf6bqj9qSH4ZpWiy2mPfOQ0jrGchcqE3j_oOTUO95gGlMecibBfyfQ== "Will you take 2 seconds to sign it -- it’d mean a ton if we could reach 10,000 signatures!")**_

---

---

I am not giving up. I introduced a bill to demand they return these funds that they acquired ILLEGALLY. I refuse to let Americans' hard-earned dollars fund abortions, and I will **ALWAYS fight for Life**.

In order to continue to do this work of defending conservative values, I need you to show that you stand with me in this fight. Help stop these taxpayer funded abortions and _**[sign my petition now](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001GigaGr4A92hqsw8tkEY7UJWCziipjPer1beXVN6W1rlz_12Z_FrmRq5ZhFz6vr3mG7WZTaZgY733X2hcwWM_ldm6bGAqXOzEGf9HfOfnwySeJgCxYZtfgvxOICEU-wiIYA1WBalc4wiT9cdN9Xv97xN-s3_6ld5tKpEKkTeIS0FCSGiv9K7rQRlSbtKlIqOJYjUuVUifBOOh_fZpvtf-6czo3sswgpAfcXxa2Skvw3bi-e_JF17unFWXcRsY6KDuqSjvihXkH5-LgVnzOhiyyDa1pA0ZMlEIxCeFvkLnrsM_62kPlPKO9KJDvsNCvLzzrfZ7r-56BMXdj6ZszYbwFoV_6No6JFfrkLuJT1zHGnCEKi-kESWsmTSwYpvUre-kPUyNrwbKo9t6b_RTx4dLnw==&c=Xv_b0l62GfzO5y2A1T_H3Z0NyqrmBgUILvXO050f0QaX0Shi8cxZpw==&ch=kf6bqj9qSH4ZpWiy2mPfOQ0jrGchcqE3j_oOTUO95gGlMecibBfyfQ== "sign my petition now")**_!

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: The First 100 Days
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501151850%2501100537%2b-%2b05-08-2021%2beNewsletter%250115396.27395230.5233880.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501151850%2501100537%2b-%2b05-08-2021%2beNewsletter%250115396.27395230.5233880.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **May 8, 2021** |
---|---|---|---
| | | It has been just over 100 days since President Joe Biden took office and started working hand-in-hand with us to tackle big issues, starting with the response to the pandemic. During this time, we passed the historic American Rescue Plan to ramp up vaccinations, deliver stimulus payments directly to families, and inject vital resources into our economy and communities across the country. While more work remains, these efforts have been [critical to Maryland](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-cardin-secure-maryland-funding-in-senate-passed-american-rescue-plan&redir_log=222465215255927), bringing much-needed relief to folks across our state. The Congress has passed and President Biden has also signed into law measures to expand supports for our small businesses, prevent Medicare payment cuts to health care providers, and ensure our Veterans get vaccinated. Through these efforts - combined with the resilience, kindness, and determination of Marylanders and people throughout the country - we are well on the road to recovery. In addition to these actions, the Senate recently took a stand against increased violence and hate aimed at members of the Asian American and Pacific Islander community by passing the COVID-19 Hate Crimes Act, which will set up a coordinated response to this insidious discrimination. Additionally, I am pleased that the Senate voted to reverse Trump's elimination of methane emissions standards that are vital to our efforts to tackle the climate emergency - this move alone will help curb climate change in just a year as we work towards bigger and broader action. We've also voted to confirm President Biden's cabinet at record speed so they can get to work.

Recently, President Biden spoke to the country before a Joint Session of Congress with confidence and conviction; seriousness and resolve; realism about the challenges we face and optimism about America's future. As the President explained, [our work has only just begun](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-biden-joint-address-to-congress&redir_log=884833816624761). We cannot simply revert to the pre-pandemic status-quo. We face far too many challenges to be running in place. Through President Biden's proposed American Families Plan and American Jobs Plan, we have the chance to meet the still untapped potential of America and invest in greater opportunities for all - from our students to our workforce - while tackling challenges like improving our infrastructure and addressing the climate crisis. I was glad to hear President Biden detail these plans in his address to Congress.

In addition to these two major initiatives, there are other challenges we must take on - from protecting the right to vote, to making long-overdue reforms to ensure racial and social justice, to taking meaningful action to stop the gun violence epidemic, to enacting comprehensive immigration reform, and much more.

The best way to tackle these challenges is by working together. President Biden has already extended a hand to all Americans to bridge the divides of this nation, and we must forge ahead. I am committed to working with the President, my colleagues, and the American people to meet the urgent demands of our time.
---
### Fighting for Reform in Law Enforcement
The jury [reached a just verdict](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-the-chauvin-verdict&redir_log=361629221264678) in the trial of Derek Chauvin, and we've just learned that a federal grand jury has indicted Chauvin and three other former Minneapolis police officers on charges that they violated George Floyd's civil rights. But nothing will bring back George Floyd or all those who should still be alive. We must move with urgency to make changes to prevent these unjust killings. One important step would be the enactment of the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act to ensure greater transparency and accountability in policing. We must pass that bill, but it is not enough. We must also make structural reforms in the way we respond to many emergency situations, and I am working with colleagues in Congress to take swift action on this issue.

I was proud to join Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott to host a [virtual roundtable](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fwatch%2flive%2f%3fv%3d519384855742193%26ref%3dwatch_permalink&redir_log=746084322123518) with community leaders and advocates to discuss the need for reforming the way we respond to certain kinds of crisis situations. Too often, a police response to a non-violent situation escalates, leaving tragedy in its wake. During the discussion, I highlighted my efforts in Congress, alongside Congresswoman Karen Bass, to improve emergency response capabilities by creating alternatives when a police response is not warranted or appropriate. That's why Congresswoman Bass and I introduced the [Community-Based Response Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-bass-introduce-new-community-based-crisis-response-legislation&redir_log=463846282944895) last session and are working to reintroduce it in the coming weeks. I was pleased to join Mayor Scott again on Friday - alongside first responders, members of law enforcement, and community partners - at the rollout of the Mayor's new 911 diversion pilot program which will make Baltimore a national model for the very type of community-based response initiative I'm pursuing with colleagues in Congress. Mayor Scott's new pilot program will ensure Baltimore residents are connected with the appropriate resources in emergency and non-emergency situations - and will allow our police to focus their efforts where they're actually needed. This approach is a step forward for true public safety.
### Taking Action in the Senate To Meet Challenges at Home and From Abroad
Over the past month, I've been working with Senate colleagues to introduce key legislation that will bolster our economy, grow jobs, and advance our country's interests. First, I teamed up with my colleague Senator Ben Sasse to reintroduce the bipartisan [Protecting American Intellectual Property Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-sasse-reintroduce-bill-to-punish-foreign-companies-individuals-for-ip-theft&redir_log=540412221684980) - legislation to impose strong economic penalties on firms and individuals involved in stealing American trade secrets, cutting-edge technologies, and intellectual property. Many foreign companies, and especially those based in China, are working overtime, often in coordination with their governments, to steal U.S. technology - damaging our economy, harming U.S. job creation, and threatening our national security in the process. This bill draws a line in the sand - outlining clear repercussions these bad actors will face if they steal vital, cutting-edge American technology.

I was also proud to join Senator Tom Carper and colleagues to [introduce](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-cardin-carper-introduce-legislation-to-reconnect-and-revitalize-communities-divided-by-highways&redir_log=891424605371125) legislation that would reconnect and revitalize areas that were harmed by the construction of portions of the Interstate Highway System, including in Maryland. For years, Baltimore's Highway to Nowhere has scarred the city, dividing Black neighborhoods and serving as a stark example of the long history of inequity in infrastructure. We must end this division by creating infrastructure that unites and connects communities. That's why I worked to author a pilot program to help tackle this issue and why I'm glad to join Senator Carper in introducing this legislation to broaden our investments so our cities can transform divisive and harmful projects into ones that promote economic opportunity. From Baltimore to cities across America, this is a key infrastructure priority, and we'll be working to get it done.

Last but not least, I was pleased to reintroduce legislation to eliminate long-term unemployment. Right now our country is facing a crisis that has stranded over 4 million Americans who have actively searched for a job for over six months without success. Millions more have given up entirely and left the workforce. In my view, every American who wants to work hard to support themselves and their family should have the opportunity to do so. This bicameral legislation, the [Long-Term Unemployment Elimination Act](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-levin-introduce-bicameral-subsidized-jobs-legislation&redir_log=648842772816830), would create a subsidized jobs program to ensure that everyone has the chance to get into the workforce. Subsidized jobs programs are a proven way to tackle this crisis, providing opportunities to Americans looking for employment while lifting up our communities and local economies. I was glad to see President Biden include this idea broadly in his American Jobs Plan and will be working to get this vital provision through Congress.
### Hearing From You
Throughout the month, I've been meeting virtually with Marylanders and community leaders from across our state to discuss the progress we've already made this year as well as the challenges ahead. On April 26th, I hosted a Telephone Town Hall to provide an update on the American Rescue Plan and answer questions from folks across Maryland. On the call, I shared the latest in our ongoing battle against COVID-19 and discussed some of the initiatives I'm pursuing in Congress to advance Maryland's priorities, including President Biden's proposed American Jobs Plan, his American Families Plan, and legislation to tackle climate change, gun safety, school funding, criminal justice reform, and voting rights.

I also had the opportunity to join Vice President Kamala Harris, Dr. Anthony Fauci, and state and local leaders for a [visit](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2fvideos%2f474066727154385%2f%3f__tn__%3d%252CO-R&redir_log=623303821751483) to the M&T Bank Stadium Mass Vaccination Site in Baltimore. We met with frontline workers and witnessed the tremendous efforts there to help get Marylanders vaccinated. We also discussed our work to secure vital funding through the American Rescue Plan to vaccinate Marylanders and bring economic support directly to workers and families across our state.

It was a good opportunity not only to review the progress we've made, but, more importantly, to chart the way ahead. That mission is only possible thanks to your input and partnership. I hope you will continue to stay engaged.

Chris Van Hollen

[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284 "Facebook")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241 "Twitter")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292 "Instagram")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326 "YouTube")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15396.27395230.5233880.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239)
|

The Congress has passed and President Biden has also signed into law measures to expand supports for our small businesses, prevent Medicare payment cuts to health care providers, and ensure our Veterans get vaccinated. Through these efforts - combined with the resilience, kindness, and determination of Marylanders and people throughout the country - we are well on the road to recovery. In addition to these actions, the Senate recently took a stand against increased violence and hate aimed at members of the Asian American and Pacific Islander community by passing the COVID-19 Hate Crimes Act, which will set up a coordinated response to this insidious discrimination. Additionally, I am pleased that the Senate voted to reverse Trump's elimination of methane emissions standards that are vital to our efforts to tackle the climate emergency - this move alone will help curb climate change in just a year as we work towards bigger and broader action. We've also voted to confirm President Biden's cabinet at record speed so they can get to work. Recently, President Biden spoke to the country before a Joint Session of Congress with confidence and conviction; seriousness and resolve; realism about the challenges we face and optimism about America's future. As the President explained, . We cannot simply revert to the pre-pandemic status-quo. We face far too many challenges to be running in place. Through President Biden's proposed American Families Plan and American Jobs Plan, we have the chance to meet the still untapped potential of America and invest in greater opportunities for all - from our students to our workforce - while tackling challenges like improving our infrastructure and addressing the climate crisis. I was glad to hear President Biden detail these plans in his address to Congress. In addition to these two major initiatives, there are other challenges we must take on - from protecting the right to vote, to making long-overdue reforms to ensure racial and social justice, to taking meaningful action to stop the gun violence epidemic, to enacting comprehensive immigration reform, and much more. The best way to tackle these challenges is by working together. President Biden has already extended a hand to all Americans to bridge the divides of this nation, and we must forge ahead. I am committed to working with the President, my colleagues, and the American people to meet the urgent demands of our time. Fighting for Reform in Law Enforcement The jury in the trial of Derek Chauvin, and we've just learned that a federal grand jury has indicted Chauvin and three other former Minneapolis police officers on charges that they violated George Floyd's civil rights. But nothing will bring back George Floyd or all those who should still be alive. We must move with urgency to make changes to prevent these unjust killings. One important step would be the enactment of the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act to ensure greater transparency and accountability in policing. We must pass that bill, but it is not enough. We must also make structural reforms in the way we respond to many emergency situations, and I am working with colleagues in Congress to take swift action on this issue. I was proud to join Baltimore Mayor Brandon Scott to host a with community leaders and advocates to discuss the need for reforming the way we respond to certain kinds of crisis situations. Too often, a police response to a non-violent situation escalates, leaving tragedy in its wake. During the discussion, I highlighted my efforts in Congress, alongside Congresswoman Karen Bass, to improve emergency response capabilities by creating alternatives when a police response is not warranted or appropriate. That's why Congresswoman Bass and I introduced the last session and are working to reintroduce it in the coming weeks. I was pleased to join Mayor Scott again on Friday - alongside first responders, members of law enforcement, and community partners - at the rollout of the Mayor's new 911 diversion pilot program which will make Baltimore a national model for the very type of community-based response initiative I'm pursuing with colleagues in Congress. Mayor Scott's new pilot program will ensure Baltimore residents are connected with the appropriate resources in emergency and non-emergency situations - and will allow our police to focus their efforts where they're actually needed. This approach is a step forward for true public safety. Taking Action in the Senate To Meet Challenges at Home and From Abroad Over the past month, I've been working with Senate colleagues to introduce key legislation that will bolster our economy, grow jobs, and advance our country's interests. First, I teamed up with my colleague Senator Ben Sasse to reintroduce the bipartisan - legislation to impose strong economic penalties on firms and individuals involved in stealing American trade secrets, cutting-edge technologies, and intellectual property. Many foreign companies, and especially those based in China, are working overtime, often in coordination with their governments, to steal U.S. technology - damaging our economy, harming U.S. job creation, and threatening our national security in the process. This bill draws a line in the sand - outlining clear repercussions these bad actors will face if they steal vital, cutting-edge American technology. I was also proud to join Senator Tom Carper and colleagues to legislation that would reconnect and revitalize areas that were harmed by the construction of portions of the Interstate Highway System, including in Maryland. For years, Baltimore's Highway to Nowhere has scarred the city, dividing Black neighborhoods and serving as a stark example of the long history of inequity in infrastructure. We must end this division by creating infrastructure that unites and connects communities. That's why I worked to author a pilot program to help tackle this issue and why I'm glad to join Senator Carper in introducing this legislation to broaden our investments so our cities can transform divisive and harmful projects into ones that promote economic opportunity. From Baltimore to cities across America, this is a key infrastructure priority, and we'll be working to get it done. Last but not least, I was pleased to reintroduce legislation to eliminate long-term unemployment. Right now our country is facing a crisis that has stranded over 4 million Americans who have actively searched for a job for over six months without success. Millions more have given up entirely and left the workforce. In my view, every American who wants to work hard to support themselves and their family should have the opportunity to do so. This bicameral legislation, the , would create a subsidized jobs program to ensure that everyone has the chance to get into the workforce. Subsidized jobs programs are a proven way to tackle this crisis, providing opportunities to Americans looking for employment while lifting up our communities and local economies. I was glad to see President Biden include this idea broadly in his American Jobs Plan and will be working to get this vital provision through Congress. Hearing From You Throughout the month, I've been meeting virtually with Marylanders and community leaders from across our state to discuss the progress we've already made this year as well as the challenges ahead. On April 26th, I hosted a Telephone Town Hall to provide an update on the American Rescue Plan and answer questions from folks across Maryland. On the call, I shared the latest in our ongoing battle against COVID-19 and discussed some of the initiatives I'm pursuing in Congress to advance Maryland's priorities, including President Biden's proposed American Jobs Plan, his American Families Plan, and legislation to tackle climate change, gun safety, school funding, criminal justice reform, and voting rights. I also had the opportunity to join Vice President Kamala Harris, Dr. Anthony Fauci, and state and local leaders for a to the M&T Bank Stadium Mass Vaccination Site in Baltimore. We met with frontline workers and witnessed the tremendous efforts there to help get Marylanders vaccinated. We also discussed our work to secure vital funding through the American Rescue Plan to vaccinate Marylanders and bring economic support directly to workers and families across our state. It was a good opportunity not only to review the progress we've made, but, more importantly, to chart the way ahead. That mission is only possible thanks to your input and partnership. I hope you will continue to stay engaged. Chris Van Hollen "Facebook" "Twitter" "Instagram" "YouTube" |

body:
We must abolish Jim Crow once and for all.
#

Hi there — we wanted to make sure you saw Mondaire’s most recent opinion piece in the _Washington Post_ , in which he urges Congress to pass legislation that would even the playing field for Black Americans and transform our democracy.
**[We’ve included the article below — and once you’re done reading, please make a contribution to Mondaire’s re-election campaign today. We need to keep him in Congress fighting for racial justice and voting rights.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/28863745/284580324/-1533274318?refcode2=90edc761-26b0-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef&refcode=em210508-1-c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODAzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGVkYzc2MS0yNmIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EoTqtBjZhzCZRUj4BKW6mz37eC-8oVXeawC8L7BAiWo=)**
* * *
_**Washington Post Opinion:** Now is the time for a Third Reconstruction — abolishing Jim Crow once and for all_
White supremacists are closer to restoring Jim Crow than at any time in memory. They are staging an assault on the right to vote: reducing early voting, restricting registration and reversing the rollout of voting by mail. We all know why: They need to entrench their diminishing hold on power by disenfranchising voters of color.
As a member of the most diverse Congress in U.S. history and one of the first openly gay Black members of Congress, I know that we are closer to building a true, multiracial democracy than ever before. But twice before in our nation’s history, we have tried to build a multiracial democracy. And twice before, white supremacists have devised ways to disenfranchise people of color.
In 1870, during Reconstruction, Congress adopted the 15th Amendment, outlawed disenfranchisement on the basis of race and created the Justice Department in part to empower Black voters.
But white supremacists soon got around that. If they couldn’t disenfranchise on the basis of race, they would disenfranchise people like me using proxies for race.
They required voters to pass arbitrary “literacy tests,” then denied Black citizens access to education. They required voters to pay poll taxes, then plundered Black communities. They barred people convicted of crimes from voting, then invented new crimes and found Black people guilty of them. And they killed whomever they had to in order to overthrow multiracial state governments.
These strategies led millions of Black Americans, including my grandparents, to flee the Jim Crow South in search of freedom. After North Carolina reelected George Henry White to Congress in 1898, the South would not elect another Black American to Congress until 1972, when Texas elected Barbara Jordan to the House and Georgia elected Andrew Young to the same body.
In 1965, during what many call the Second Reconstruction, Congress passed the Voting Rights Act, which renewed our democracy’s opposition to racial discrimination. The law enacted real protections to safeguard voting rights for Black people across the South for the first time. And it restored the Justice Department to its original primary role of ensuring that we were not systematically denied the right to vote.
But in recent years, a far-right majority on the Supreme Court has enabled white supremacists to circumvent the Voting Rights Act, striking down the strongest protections — as in Shelby County v. Holder — and weakening others.
We now find ourselves at a crossroads. For the first time in 10 years, the Democratic Party controls Congress and the White House. Building a multiracial democracy will not be easy. But if we do not act now, it may soon be impossible.
Now is the time for a Third Reconstruction — one that abolishes Jim Crow once and for all. That means making voting as easy as possible for everyone, by establishing automatic voter registration, protecting our voter rolls from purges, and ensuring universally accessible ballots for seniors, people with disabilities and anyone else who needs an accommodation. That means restoring that right to the 5.2 million people, disproportionately Black and brown, who have been disenfranchised because of felony convictions.
Second, we must end partisan gerrymandering of congressional districts. That’s the distorting system that has evolved to permit politicians to choose their voters rather than voters to choose their politicians. Congress’s power to right this wrong is beyond dispute. As even Justice Antonin Scalia wrote for the Supreme Court in 2013, the Constitution’s elections clause gives Congress the “authority to provide a complete code for congressional elections.” Until we end partisan gerrymandering, a declining White minority will wield it to deny communities of color the representation they deserve — packing them into safe districts to waste their votes or spreading them out to diffuse their power. Meanwhile, QAnon conspiracy theorists will continue coasting to victory in general elections after prevailing in Republican primaries. These are distortions of our democracy.
Third, we must end the pernicious power of Big Money, which converts the racial wealth gap into a political power gap. An under-told truth of our politics is that the donor class often anoints the candidates who make it on the ballot. That donor class is overwhelmingly White and conservative. And that big-money gatekeeping disproportionately excludes candidates of color, who rely more on small donations.
Public campaign financing would amplify the voices of communities of color. The For the People Act, commonly known as H.R.1 or S.1, would match every dollar donated up to $200 with $6 raised from penalties on corporations that have broken the law. A $50 donation would become a $350 donation. In New York City, a limited, small-donor matching program has already fostered more representative donors and candidates for city offices.
This transformation of our democracy will not happen overnight. But Congress can bring us closer than ever before by passing the For the People Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act — uncompromised. If we squander this opportunity, future generations will not — and should not — forgive us.
* * *

I am holding Joe Biden and Kamala Harris' feet to the fire - their failure of leadership at our southern border cannot go unanswered.
[](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001--yUY1L1hQ6LKSrosnoz2JTyFnzDfeTjJUFttc-z11ZXeaF-aUnZFBAqKTn7oyivX4K103L-P4HhIXD2IfJC8wlP8Mj2ktgdQ8mf9kxQk7XrhDa39E87wpob95nO1Tt463LPYeBFzuWGidV3Cr9pqvbD88Xsnc_bH1MZ9d6y2AIXMcV3jIlyWH6GshRa75sPEWmOq2pGVKHqEfZyuiE0qhPswZLAGD4LJoxYzQH8aY7YciupLNSJKsjtguTKzMIa9jw7pRJo52UyAlFwNpdaJS8xU_XWqG4GGF8W5V1IwyF2XoU7bAXZIbrYJe7obUBhFfO7eh52-demXdmWJ-e8vFjtskOwLqDQfnxNq5Yh2e6WRocVTSkKr1NdpO1yhpBnzPSGQg08iwrepZUBmxI0bjwxhCQxsK10Ryvx20uVAUn1BT_ZXF73YKt8ZunTvM0zZ6WOHmreJH0=&c=zEcvAfA_tlk-MZuy39kxCxosoYnRKS4pqOkaW_7MbygXOhOHnZEOWw==&ch=6ZiLuL_51OxT6HmgEfnZ4XKyXYvObbrw3scwgnXxdgN1giKtHWrOng==)
I spoke up. And now every left-wing group, along with the BILLION dollar liberal fundraising machine funded by the big donors in New York and California, are coming after me.
And let me tell you, I am not backing down. With the radical left in control of Washington, conservative governors are now the last line of defense.
It's time to fight back. Can I count on you to help me stop them?
****[**Fight Back Against The Left**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001--yUY1L1hQ6LKSrosnoz2JTyFnzDfeTjJUFttc-z11ZXeaF-aUnZFBAqKTn7oyivX4K103L-P4HhIXD2IfJC8wlP8Mj2ktgdQ8mf9kxQk7XrhDa39E87wpob95nO1Tt463LPYeBFzuWGidV3Cr9pqvbD88Xsnc_bH1MZ9d6y2AIXMcV3jIlyWH6GshRa75sPEWmOq2pGVKHqEfZyuiE0qhPswZLAGD4LJoxYzQH8aY7YciupLNSJKsjtguTKzMIa9jw7pRJo52UyAlFwNpdaJS8xU_XWqG4GGF8W5V1IwyF2XoU7bAXZIbrYJe7obUBhFfO7eh52-demXdmWJ-e8vFjtskOwLqDQfnxNq5Yh2e6WRocVTSkKr1NdpO1yhpBnzPSGQg08iwrepZUBmxI0bjwxhCQxsK10Ryvx20uVAUn1BT_ZXF73YKt8ZunTvM0zZ6WOHmreJH0=&c=zEcvAfA_tlk-MZuy39kxCxosoYnRKS4pqOkaW_7MbygXOhOHnZEOWw==&ch=6ZiLuL_51OxT6HmgEfnZ4XKyXYvObbrw3scwgnXxdgN1giKtHWrOng==)** **

Friend, I am the Governor of Iowa. My #1 responsibility is to protect the health and safety of Iowans, **and it 's why I am standing up to Biden's failed leadership at the border.**
The Biden Administration is HEARTLESS for causing this humanitarian crisis at our border. I am not afraid to call it what it is... Joe Biden and his Liberal allies opened our borders, and are pushing a far left agenda.
Now, Democrats want to silence me, and they will spend and do whatever it takes to win.
If every conservative like you chips in just $1 it will make a huge difference, and send a message to the liberal elite.
Conservative governors like me are fighting back against Joe Biden's radical left agenda, but we can't do it alone. name: Jake Oakman
subject: “The best budget in recent memory”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
We couldn’t be any more excited about what the future holds for Hoosier education under this transformative, historic budget, and Governor Holcomb.. | |
---
|
---
Friend,

_**“Long overdue.”**_

_**“The best budget in recent memory.”**_

_**“A step in the right direction.”**_

These are just a handful of examples of the incredible feedback we’re seeing in the media in response to the recent boost in Hoosier education funding spearheaded by Governor Holcomb.

**[[READ] OPINION: Hoosiers Lead the Voucher Way ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=cd3ce7feb872422fbc35fa87974a8e83&_e=G_ctpFz_Px6-sBfTBxYvKTmEP5pk5bHGObUvZLaX3YShOf565gqCdkjVq37vS1qYAm8-E8cBkJXxt7vAeqs8gvigMZJEme4Wqzo9-P9U12Taj8Ux2l1lXmxNKeDwDbpTCo81OtYeVZMBuxG3nrWMLf49kLfJ7CBgA4yWiK8mVxyOY_kWOTJHZ_afMSQ8eOEsd4I3m6OQcpPWUzkpX8Ygc1xkQ6RYJPrDE5MhceOdDwQAjd-b9QJVtx9vnaMljt2bfnWFWuCjSc0LiQALq90Nfq8ENWDzSZER-Z_NJQOGlXfT2GR2RNEgw6hv2Iljr85t5cGY5M-aabsz9jYFskktNdPCNeGaZVMfC_uNpnneffhTHX_BpQAJg0cxiteQDF1Gla_cmtDpWQspiAV2bQyf9L0Ac9UyxsbE_JvwyXsdN7MPc6NbViHJ5wbwG8JPyxr6o_MfeSqO_eGjAqNHEdxitIELIKdm-WwYjMQLkGffVjXF0QuDkZiznbiXyCUFXw01y4CyKYd0zHBB209AdryXvC2kVhNnE9sR3cBmfzrJIF0%3D)**
**The bottom line:** _“Once again Hoosiers are leading the way in establishing an education system that gives parents and children a choice.”_ — The Editorial Board, The Wall Street Journal

**[[READ] Teacher salaries to increase under funding approved for Indiana schools](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=cd3ce7feb872422fbc35fa87974a8e83&_e=G_ctpFz_Px6-sBfTBxYvKefKQtTW6AP8HqaqOe5YbPugouZbedT7Yp7GFmum5cLWV3ySizisZwMCM70IFvvfY58UDHKlqP9qwc1rej7_iu9bQuQ-lmX8bnqp336b_36edf-jix8PIGvoRwMHjy9jKR3QjAaNrJDtbq5e213yj33OqFcz_rciPMvywEzPpuFFHzvP86Idmgiqi4iQPdX6gq4MPumYYgWi9SvD0d_NJYdKl8eAqw_t00iEtvJNMnJnif58UXwhvVVZCnEwlN52N-vlMJpiCWbZnSarzKA8J_DmE4XOHVUf-foCNTJ3T_V-L-UntMWgJYbvTy2vCYc7eD3fjsWDrkpI4ywWRFFwzuwcy5S69Q-WN6xP1eE-G4Jm__Yq5rqfUfy0CWLU6RXiVfbGgY_moa8jqhKJBqu9P8hKR2OpZQ0i2iKPAJ2CtCXgC_3EWLnKff8K7-7tuJclKaqKNxPC05t5MrqCS3FVTSZbJ-4O-Jdlf9ZWEWLZVJChQR30qdXnAPdihiRydiVnfLKPWtvG5PY9M8KCvk9agajiWL65kz5a-O7JO4j0IdiAwiwTmUjmdFSDS6h6b3WpqJkhrxj0HPBd1kHrGXAWx-Q%3D)**
**The bottom line:** _“Teacher salaries in the Hoosier state are set to increase after lawmakers approved a new two-year state budget — one teachers say is long overdue. Indiana lawmakers on Thursday gave the greenlight to a $37 billion state spending plan which includes raises and a higher pay benchmark for new teachers. The 4.5% funding boost would be each of the next two years.”_ –– WDRB

**[[READ] Indiana schools see long-awaited funding increases for special education, English learners](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=cd3ce7feb872422fbc35fa87974a8e83&_e=G_ctpFz_Px6-sBfTBxYvKbg1iE8K75HXW1ZjW6bnc4y9IjAUh_72bnEmPwwYp1_0oWZVzSgNJlcEqey6qrmhHukarGZl3iNR0Oo1BDdrVq4Lh89RjrCnXK-CCYVPweWrNuhA_a4J6HPEv8ouqrbqNLO20rIIBUUuePKLGHs2J35EaM10slMltkR9bKioUkEAC2Rv-EGk69Fo-v6ddVZo1Fyx4JB-M2lP_l7YM289NmQDxZye3074xynnTC02DvueBR84hkIRFGAwtVOM4TMUmxSjEhp6Ja3tF-LX7dRFzKTlPRMJfhg_TraJh5Z9dhxVHDgnUmnKjzCpfOQ8he_7bylyiw-4Z0HNBT1nNaUXcn6QKmxLh9sAPfAyjm2sW0RZxJObIu2M0j7HCfL5ndk9Xpzh74uFgx43HFQ8avyzU5FEjQvuUUC7a0Ym0-Gi4zakmszpwWvs2G0rrGrkPL-zdATrWgooIG9B6f6us6R0dK5SRXOlqS3DZdMxc0gsO7N4KE9cOWLVtaljc0QFAvBEBzcSGJFYQMuwwUmLhHFYkbAkuysLmzvh0_dA10Bs0vmT-NGBmTUE7EmfaMC-TNrrMw%3D%3D)**
**The bottom line:** _“Indiana schools will receive more money for special education programs over the next two years, an increase educators say is long overdue and applaud for coming at a crucial moment for students. Lawmakers increased funding for special education by $196 million, an average 10.8% yearly increase, in the state’s new two-year budget, part of a massive increase in overall education spending thanks in part to an infusion of federal stimulus funds. They also approved an 18% increase worth $5 million each year for English language learners.”_ –– Chalkbeat

**[[READ] LETTERS TO THE EDITOR: Public School Funding Boost Welcome
](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=cd3ce7feb872422fbc35fa87974a8e83&_e=G_ctpFz_Px6-sBfTBxYvKVkeL6J2nEJHPvl-nOOI5uQuZQY9ngywEzlqedQOycG8NgPjub15G_lVVNbIbTHy1p-lfUDNWGM43GeOv0Vo57BgY_wJRF7tCw3NQObL1qcObHNffT5poigPDH88SmB2mwKknDQ1xOehwmCSp85vnjDMhoy9czXEdO0xw0W1Eq9lJrgyKTaoATmvUd63TuD3Nj1YRhZEaPBP7ftWVwPVs_Ecv-xKLrT42ZDQdDmWTeQYsbk0b5j2RnGJtsOt-DEXfbEZsHjB3E7zSvVGQsKnJUWWnhDLw-7b6cJ8UQFvuOGs0srw3vg8GLShhZaeMwHGVU7izTMviRflusftCk9TwQB4gJl3Ys6dMYe5gOGtVTc05UWufx_YR5MwSVEEW5F-72uVfVR0PDtoxEdxQ2dG78vMcv8GKR0DycIN4VwZ_hDxun1vabuQnHQhcpzUFTl616OjpEB0gCa9fc0UWAHg87rP3UKXoCvZDz3q43fUgfwWKHrDlRpXzCWhk2UtKuCApTzMsdzOCbpI8OHrTkpeUD0n_7fNPK745ngBpG9LS0MO)The bottom line:** _“(The budget) provides a substantial increase for teacher pay. A 9.1% increase in tuition support means a $1.2 billion increase for schools over the next two years. This is huge because the bill also states that 45% of that money will be used to address the $600 million shortage in teacher pay.”_ — Mark Felix, Greater Clark Education Association president, News and Tribune

**[[READ] OPINION: New Indiana education funding is a step in the right direction](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=cd3ce7feb872422fbc35fa87974a8e83&_e=G_ctpFz_Px6-sBfTBxYvKYT30ZwJNm9LdA_ffNyBrv9ZBsRTj5D-4Ums_RmZXqI6LZeID0_vU-X9092zJgVFhoCzo3gk-esBsD6YpGN85uTELzL0KlHJR9zUKYHL3KKsESqzjKUeHJOp5LidMb0RUI1u8J3XJc9SzTk1Q1BcXtBerd-ruF3axsm_-J2fIy_KGZ7fcGOOUEd3TjvMbu0w-DkGqDkkyWU3_YPtSAtC-O8jUb3OcNStRKuO2f8_5uow2tqRpYuSX4Yhz9t0GnyUShbs5SbfZoc5PNDdlZiESrw9Zu_VK3A0JlZ3qavHvD-e08wzZc7rT_7Z6ormcaLh8nZHdd_JB9Sc4mJfBOZfpggECoDDB2_SozVUAmfmEjcZAnLlIfyyrUbKD5wVqXlkC5mebYE29ljlCLV0hR454VbBx2TJYWDDpZEPnvLjufIsVpAx7Gg98SE3WoUYoVn9QmxN9ExUgj4BvulJaKkP8Lr4kRkEMXQbGv75XioaN1ogj_H98fJvEV_MayXZrRZB3w%3D%3D)**
**The bottom line:** _“K-12 funding has been drastically increased compared to what was expected in the budget. The boost in education funding raises the total amount allocated to K-12 public education to about $16 billion combined over those two years, with just over a 4% increase in spending each year. A significant portion of this boost — $600 million — will go to increasing the salaries of K-12 teachers. This is essential because Indiana’s average teacher salary lags well behind neighboring states.”_ –– Noah Moore, Indiana Daily Student

Friend, this overwhelmingly positive response from the media aligns with what Governor Holcomb and lawmakers from both sides of the aisle have been saying:

_**This budget is a WIN-WIN-WIN for Hoosier students, teachers, and parents.**_

We couldn’t be any more excited about what the future holds for Hoosier education under this transformative, historic budget, and Governor Holcomb is proud to have the support of Hoosiers along the way.

Thank you,

Jake Oakman

[[READ] OPINION: Hoosiers Lead the Voucher Way ]() The bottom line: “Once again Hoosiers are leading the way in establishing an education system that gives parents and children a choice.” — The Editorial Board, The Wall Street Journal [[READ] Teacher salaries to increase under funding approved for Indiana schools]() The bottom line: “Teacher salaries in the Hoosier state are set to increase after lawmakers approved a new two-year state budget — one teachers say is long overdue. Indiana lawmakers on Thursday gave the greenlight to a $37 billion state spending plan which includes raises and a higher pay benchmark for new teachers. The 4.5% funding boost would be each of the next two years.” –– WDRB [[READ] Indiana schools see long-awaited funding increases for special education, English learners]() The bottom line: “Indiana schools will receive more money for special education programs over the next two years, an increase educators say is long overdue and applaud for coming at a crucial moment for students. Lawmakers increased funding for special education by $196 million, an average 10.8% yearly increase, in the state’s new two-year budget, part of a massive increase in overall education spending thanks in part to an infusion of federal stimulus funds. They also approved an 18% increase worth $5 million each year for English language learners.” –– Chalkbeat [[READ] LETTERS TO THE EDITOR: Public School Funding Boost Welcome ]( bottom line: “(The budget) provides a substantial increase for teacher pay. A 9.1% increase in tuition support means a $1.2 billion increase for schools over the next two years. This is huge because the bill also states that 45% of that money will be used to address the $600 million shortage in teacher pay.” — Mark Felix, Greater Clark Education Association president, News and Tribune [[READ] OPINION: New Indiana education funding is a step in the right direction]() The bottom line: “K-12 funding has been drastically increased compared to what was expected in the budget. The boost in education funding raises the total amount allocated to K-12 public education to about $16 billion combined over those two years, with just over a 4% increase in spending each year. A significant portion of this boost — $600 million — will go to increasing the salaries of K-12 teachers. This is essential because Indiana’s average teacher salary lags well behind neighboring states.” –– Noah Moore, Indiana Daily Student Friend, this overwhelmingly positive response from the media aligns with what Governor Holcomb and lawmakers from both sides of the aisle have been saying: This budget is a WIN-WIN-WIN for Hoosier students, teachers, and parents. We couldn’t be any more excited about what the future holds for Hoosier education under this transformative, historic budget, and Governor Holcomb is proud to have the support of Hoosiers along the way. Thank you, Jake Oakman | | | | | Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana. © Copyright 2021. Friend,

clean_body: | Friend, I have some concerning news: We're still 14 responses short of what we need to fight back against the Arizona GOP's sham election audit. Before it's too late: Not only are extremist Republicans like Kelli Ward trying to steal the election from President Biden – but they've also hired QAnon-supporting conspiracy theorists to "recount" millions of ballots. I mean, friend – we just heard their "big strategy" is to look for bamboo in the ballot papers, all because they heard a conspiracy theory that China tried to rig our election for Joe Biden. It's completely ridiculous – but if we don't speak up and call this audit the sham that it is, then the GOP may succeed in casting doubt on President Biden's victory. That's why I need to hear from you so urgently. Please, we only have a few hours left before we close this petition, so speak out right now: Will you condemn the Arizona GOP's sham "election audit" and defend our democracy against these baseless, outrageous attacks? | | | | Thank you, Marco \---------- Forwarded message ---------- From: Rachel Maddow Alert (via Team Lopez) Date: Fri, May 7, 2021 Subject: re: Maddow's message for Democrats | | | BREAKING UPDATE FOR Friend : RACHEL MADDOW BLASTS ARIZONA GOP FOR HIRING QANON-PROMOTING, PRO-TRUMP FIRM TO CONDUCT "SECRET" ELECTION AUDIT SPEAK OUT NOW: WILL YOU CONDEMN THE ARIZONA GOP'S SHAM "ELECTION AUDIT" AND DEFEND OUR DEMOCRACY? | | | | | Friend, this may be the most outrageous attack on our democracy yet. Extremists in the Arizona GOP – led by Kelli Ward – are currently recounting millions of ballots in a sham "election audit" – all to overturn President Joe Biden's historic victory in Arizona! Even worse? They've hired a QAnon-supporting, conspiracy theorist group to conduct the recount in secret. Let's be clear: They're not trying to make our elections more secure. They're still trying to push Trump's Big Lie, steal the election from President Biden and rig the system so Democrats can never win in Arizona again. And we need to fight back NOW. Democrats across the country are speaking out against this anti-democratic attack on our elections – but we still don't know where you stand, friend. We're counting on another 19 concerned Democrats from 18038 to make their voices heard before tonight's midnight deadline, so please: WILL YOU CONDEMN THE ARIZONA GOP'S SHAM "ELECTION AUDIT" AND DEFEND OUR DEMOCRACY? | | | | 11:59 P.M. DEADLINE | COMPLETION RATE IN 18038: 98.4% Thanks for making your voice heard. – Team Lopez | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Peter,

At the Conservative Political Action Conference in Orlando, President Trump took the stage and declared that, “We went through a journey like nobody else... we began it together four years ago, and it is far from being over.”

**I couldn’t agree with that sentiment more**.

First, Pelosi’s party in Congress gave us a “covid relief” bill without any relief for covid.
Now, they’re trying to give us an “infrastructure plan” that doesn’t include plans for infrastructure! Their reckless spending in disguise nonsense must be stopped. Will you help fiscal conservatives like me defeat the plan by chipping in now?: [**DONATE**](http://links.email.nancymace.org/u/click?_t=b7e0c98b86c6435ea03767402a51b3fc&_m=532f800fcfbc41d9937fdec4c9a28ed2&_e=mitQPSswGtBK3IInSD9dUKiUtmIqprpAYoT7jlc00DL7kG8929UhSYZJP0FO1oap9BLhF0mbWZOWxZCm2eXVsWBAPq5W8gGqn2SE0s5-TqT3Gy9GmDieIgQ7NiSNwy4vIibI0-OXNr0B-N1hdrlwVnTC19Eu0QlUCvmHRVWVggWaeUChnoLAtBSzdev_6UbKEa9Vj8S0ELtU3SkzvXzFLEp5gQroSinJATOLmR1uNeeCXrASab9Yd9Pd8f3WAP_pSKzdO0z2vDNmMWF3MZAZrwWZbo6tiNh4jNA_IantMvtBHCWLpmsSU6HhYGKgCDu19XPjZuwW8NHeeM4Cfzz6mthscL6SovRl4ukHioRTS6WPNIF9rF8LAyItdbEVxiBP6RqdpETPInNQzrJ31YJFM87IxtM9Kv6hCqqCenBJDabSzDhwy0wUbdo-VTsF6quGGQKBDABCurm8yGyhfdXdYAbinVf1nXt77rZPHatoHg_UPWPBbLPL8nHWYpPB9kqd) As a member of the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee in Congress, I want to put the Lowcountry’s needs first -- our roads, bridges and ports and planes. _**But the left is hijacking a bipartisan issue and transforming it into a socialist wish list. **_ They’re proposing:
* $20 billion for environmental justice. Not infrastructure!
* $175 billion for electric vehicles subsidies. Not infrastructure!
* $100 billion to make school lunches "Greener." -- which, if you noticed, is also NOT INFRASTRUCTURE!

_Another $400 billion bails out Medicaid programs, $600 billion goes to corporate subsidies, and the list goes on and on and as you might have imagined, very little of it is what any normal American would consider to be "infrastructure." _ These are exactly the kind of say-one-thing-and-do-another tricks that have made people so upset with Congress and I will not stand for it. They’re even giving this whopper a new name and calling it a “Jobs” plan now because the Left knows they’ve been caught. **If you're with me, please join me to demand an infrastructure plan that actually aims to improve American infrastructure by chipping in now:[https://secure.winred.com/nancymace/action-2315665/](http://links.email.nancymace.org/u/click?_t=b7e0c98b86c6435ea03767402a51b3fc&_m=532f800fcfbc41d9937fdec4c9a28ed2&_e=mitQPSswGtBK3IInSD9dUKiUtmIqprpAYoT7jlc00DL7kG8929UhSYZJP0FO1oap9BLhF0mbWZOWxZCm2eXVsWBAPq5W8gGqn2SE0s5-TqT3Gy9GmDieIgQ7NiSNwy4vIibI0-OXNr0B-N1hdrlwVnTC19Eu0QlUCvmHRVWVggWaeUChnoLAtBSzdev_6UbKEa9Vj8S0ELtU3SkzvXzFLEp5gQroSinJATOLmR1uNeeCXrASab9Yd9Pd8f3WAP_pSKzdO0z2vDNmMWF3MZAZrwWZbo6tiNh4jNA_IantMvtNCg7qci79Ior0mgx0-W7ijz6VhtF2Hmg_iNWpuV0vQ81Xr5JhK_WYIQnN0niiSNdpPUxLwvgE3fCd0Gc_3LG10CHDIFTApRIS1Fw5O8FAlLFR3yUIDxmS5yBw5sacftpxiH3ejAJiO3W4F2wbnMxP6_7AVILdfmj35Pxslhwdjzt2p2wIfyVa8ygnPXwPYD-Zrq8VykLy0KmmhM22zvEy)** Let's get to work on the real solutions. clean_body: I’m getting really tired of the same old same old Washington distraction tactics. First, Pelosi’s party in Congress gave us a “covid... Friend, I’m getting really tired of the same old same old Washington distraction tactics. First, Pelosi’s party in Congress gave us a “covid relief” bill without any relief for covid. Now, they’re trying to give us an “infrastructure plan” that doesn’t include plans for infrastructure! Their reckless spending in disguise nonsense must be stopped. Will you help fiscal conservatives like me defeat the plan by chipping in now?: As a member of the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee in Congress, I want to put the Lowcountry’s needs first -- our roads, bridges and ports and planes. But the left is hijacking a bipartisan issue and transforming it into a socialist wish list. They’re proposing: $20 billion for environmental justice. Not infrastructure! $175 billion for electric vehicles subsidies. Not infrastructure! $100 billion to make school lunches "Greener." -- which, if you noticed, is also NOT INFRASTRUCTURE! Another $400 billion bails out Medicaid programs, $600 billion goes to corporate subsidies, and the list goes on and on and as you might have imagined, very little of it is what any normal American would consider to be "infrastructure." These are exactly the kind of say-one-thing-and-do-another tricks that have made people so upset with Congress and I will not stand for it. They’re even giving this whopper a new name and calling it a “Jobs” plan now because the Left knows they’ve been caught. If you're with me, please join me to demand an infrastructure plan that actually aims to improve American infrastructure by chipping in now:** Let's get to work on the real solutions. clean_body: President Trump warned us what would happen if Democrats took power. After 100 days of Joe Biden, it's clear there will be NOTHING "moderate" about this Administration. President Trump warned us what would happen if Democrats took power. After 100 days of Joe Biden, it's clear there will be NOTHING "moderate" about this Administration. Unless the GOP can mobilize our grassroots supporters like never before, I'm afraid it's only going to get worse. What we 're seeing play out is a fight for the soul of country. I'm personally asking you to rush immediate resources to help the GOP win this fight! == | == == == == == == == == == == == == == == If conservatives like you don't step up to fight while the Left openly attacks our liberties and leads our country down the path to socialism, we won't have a chance of making up lost ground in 2022. I'll be honest, to defeat the Left and their fundraising Machine, it's going to take real commitment. The liberal media is saying that Republicans are DONE now that big corporations have sided with the Left and pulled their support. The truth is that Republicans rely on continuous small dollar donations from grassroots supporters like you. == Taking back the House is President Trump's top priority, and he can't afford to have a SINGLE Patriot stay on the sidelines in this mission. Your continuous support will be what takes the GOP across the finish line when it counts. Thank you for joining the fight at this crucial moment. Newt Gingrich == == ** | Contributions to National Victory Action Fund are not deductible as charitable donations for federal income tax purposes. Paid for by the National Victory Action Fund. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 2024 3rd Ave North, Ste 212 Birmingham, AL 35203 == | | NVAF | PO BOX 9891, Arlington, VA 22219 | | Sent by mailto:action@conservativevictory2020.net powered by

name: Mike Collier
subject: Thank you for joining our campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Collier Committee
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP30/1/89772/images/Logo%20%282%29.png]
Friend,

I’m seriously considering running to be the next lieutenant governor of our great state of Texas, and I want to thank you personally for joining the team.

Decades of failed GOP leadership has brought us to this point. Our leaders continue to fail to meet the scale of the challenges we’re facing today.

Our public schools are underfunded, our economy is leaving behind working-class families, our property taxes are too high, and our rickety energy grid is long past its due date for repair.

Texans are tired of the dysfunction, corruption, and failed leadership of Greg Abbott, Ken Patrick, and especially, Dan Patrick. I am ready to deliver the kind of change that will bring integrity, compassion, and our Texas values back to our government.

We can hold them accountable for their failed leadership, but only if we beat them, so I need to know that you’ll have my back if I decide to enter the race. So I’m asking you personally:

Will you add your name today to join the fight? We’re ready to build a movement grounded in compassion for every Texan in every county in Texas, but it’s going to take all of us to do it.

JOIN THE FIGHT: https://secure.ngpvan.com/1o6ZnkJEdUyA-hYmfzGw6g2

We’re going to get out there and fight for every vote in every single county. (Yes, all 254 of ‘em!)

I have no doubt that this will be an uphill battle. But with your support, we can build a future for Texas that puts people first.

Thanks for standing by my side.

— Mike Collier
The Collier Committee
P.O. Box 14648
Humble, TX 77347
United States
[https://collierfortexas.com/] |
Paid for by the Collier Committee
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . We believe that emails are the best way to connect with our supporters and keep you up-to-date. However, if you’d still like to receive fewer emails, fill out this form here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2abP63YYcUqXP5eHRkxWIQ2]. If you wish to no longer receive these messages, please unsubscribe. [https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/5aef1f89-e296-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef/9fc01107-41b0-eb11-85aa-0050f237abef?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTc3MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZmMwMTEwNy00MWIwLWViMTEtODVhYS0wMDUwZjIzN2FiZWYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CxLLwa8KBdSfnxB7mOF_KxQ8q_9Di2I3iHUQR44M1Z0=&id=109059170]
